title: Director of Early Childhood Education
notes: Large urban district (~19K students). SLPS Early Childhood Education office on third floor of Central Office. Also serves: Aubrey Schmoll (Early Learning Manager) and Latasha Anderson (EC Coordinator). District operates extensive Head Start and MO Preschool Program (MPP) classrooms.

title: Principal, Great Beginnings Early Education Center
notes: Major suburban KC district (~17K students). Recently expanded Great Beginnings ECEC to two locations including Paradise Park; Dr. Patti White joined 2024 from Independence SD where she ran Head Start. Uses Project Construct curriculum framework. Kendra Burke serves as District Program Coordinator (816.986.1514) overseeing early childhood.

title: Principal, Liberty Early Childhood Center
notes: Large KC north metro district. Liberty ECC at 9600 NE 79th St offers full-day program. Ashley Assman appointed as Asst Principal effective July 2026 (LPS Board, March 2026) - leadership transition signal. ECC expansion in process.

title: Director of Early Childhood (effective July 2026)
notes: St Louis County district (~10K students). Major leadership transition: Erin Amador appointed Director of EC for 2026-27, replacing Tiffany Schwaegel who moves to Asst Supt of Student Services. District recently added full-day EC at Bierbaum Elementary. Email pattern: lastname+firstinitial@msdr9.org (confirmed via callnewspapers article). Schwaegel's outgoing email schwaegelt@msdr9.org.

title: Principal, Kirkwood Early Childhood Center
notes: Affluent St Louis County district. KECC at 100 N Sappington Rd serves age 2 - kindergarten eligible with full-day and half-day options plus ECSE via SSD. Recent news (2024-25): expanded enrollment and financial aid to make preschool more accessible.

title: Principal, MRH Early Childhood Center
notes: Small St Louis County district. MRH ECC at 2810 Burgess Ave uses Reggio Emilia-inspired 'School as Studio' approach. Asst Principal: Dr. Robert Ricker. Vince Estrada (vince.estrada@mrhschools.net) handles enrollment as Director of Student Services. Email convention: first.last@mrhschools.net.

title: Superintendent
notes: Small St Louis County district. Bayless ECC accredited preschool uses Project Construct curriculum and Conscious Discipline. ECC has dedicated principal role for EC+K but staff page requires JS to load names. Superintendent listed as fallback contact; Bayless ECC serves ages 3-6 tuition program plus PAT birth-5.

title: Program Director, Grace Early Childhood Care & Education Center
notes: South KC metro district. Grace ECC at 614 W Mill St in Belton serves birth-PreK with childcare, preschool, ECSE. Loveland joined July 2023 from KCK Public Schools EC dept (9 yrs); MEd Educational Leadership from Pittsburg State 2012. District uses Conscious Discipline SEL framework.

title: Early Childhood, Yellowjacket Early Learning Center
notes: Urban KC district at 8434 Paseo Blvd (~2,400 students). Operates Yellowjacket Early Learning Center for PreK. Dr. Dawn Smith serves as Asst Supt Talent/Operations and Meagan Patterson as Asst Supt Student Support and Success (oversight role). Principal email lead: mafleming@center.k12.mo.us.

title: Executive Director of PK-6
notes: Growing KC north metro district. Smithville ECC + Warrior Preschool operate as part of district. Harwood holds EdS Leadership & Policy Design from Mizzou. Email pattern verified: lastname+firstinitial@smithville.k12.mo.us. District has Warrior preschool branding.

title: Director, Maryville Early Childhood Center (MECC)
notes: NW Missouri district (~1,400 students). MECC at 418 E Second St combines preschool and kindergarten in one center. 3 of 6 preschool classrooms tuition-paid, 3 free (likely MPP/Title I). Email pattern verified from MECC secretary listing (jonesk@maryviller2.com pattern - using maryville.k12.mo.us domain).

title: Superintendent
notes: Franklin County district. Operates dedicated Early Learning Center combining ECSE, PAT, and all in-town preschools. Family Resource Center houses EC programs. Prior ELC principal Dr. Dawn Hellebusch (2018-19); current ELC principal not surfaced via public sources - need direct enrichment. Superintendent listed as decision-maker fallback.

title: Assistant Superintendent
notes: Phelps County district (~1,600 students PK-12). Webster oversees curriculum/instruction. Supt Dr. Merlyn Johnson (mj@stjschools.org). District emphasizes 'relevant, rigorous, viable curriculum' per strategic statement. No dedicated EC director surfaced; Webster as Asst Supt is curriculum-and-instruction decision-maker.

title: Assistant Superintendent (Curriculum, Assessment, Student Learning, PD)
notes: Park Hills district in St. Francois County. England oversees curriculum, assessment, student learning, professional development, and safety - primary C&I decision-maker. Supt: Dr. Zech Payne. Two elementary schools serve preschool through grade 6.

title: Superintendent
notes: Bootheel Mississippi County district. Title I-eligible community. Operates preschool and PAT (Lisa Minner serves as PAT Director at Warren E Hearnes Elementary). Small district structure - superintendent appropriate primary contact. Hearnes ES is PreK-elementary site.

title: Superintendent
notes: Small rural Butler County district (~647 students PK-12). No dedicated EC director - small district. Supt is primary curriculum decision-maker for PreK.

title: Superintendent
notes: Very small Bootheel district in New Madrid County (~215 students total across 2 schools including 1 preschool). 100% Title I community. Superintendent appropriate target - need first name confirmation. District spends $15,632/student.

title: Elementary Principal
notes: Bootheel Mississippi County district. Scruggs runs PK-elementary site. Recent superintendent transition: Dr. Aaron Cornman / Dustin Hicks (acornman@eastprairie.org). Small district, elementary principal is primary EC/curriculum contact.

title: Principal, Wildwood Elementary (PK-5)
notes: Southwest MO district in Jasper/Lawrence/Newton counties (~693 students). Wildwood ES (296 students PK-5) is the only elementary - Feather oversees PreK programming directly. Supt Phil Lewis (plewis@sarcoxieschools.com). Ashley Lambeth is Federal Programs Director (potential Title I PK contact).

title: Principal, Tiger Paw Early Childhood Center
notes: Fast-growing SW MO Christian County district. Winckler hired April 2025 to replace Dr. Elizabeth Dawson who moved to Executive Director of Special Services (elizabethdawson@ozarktigers.org as backup contact). Tiger Paw secured $100K in grants in 2024 to add two classrooms and pay tuition for preschool families - active EC expansion signal.

title: Director of Early Childhood
notes: Tourist-economy SW MO district. Ray in second year as Director of EC. Recent expansion: 2-year-old preschool classroom opened; 2026-27 preschool registration opened early indicating growing EC interest. Ray holds doctorate from Lindenwood. Cedar Ridge Primary houses EC programs.

title: Superintendent
notes: Lake of the Ozarks district (Camden County). Early Childhood Center at 1571 Bagnell Dam Blvd, Lake Ozark serves 86 PreK students with 7 teachers. ECC director name not surfaced via public sources; superintendent serves as primary curriculum decision-maker pending enrichment. Supt Dr. Laura Nelson is curriculum-active per Supt Updates.

title: Early Childhood Process Coordinator
notes: Central MO district (~3,500 students). MAJOR EC EXPANSION: New Early Childhood Center under construction at 1047 S Maguire St with 13 classrooms (groundbreaking June 2024). Strong buying signal for ECE curriculum. Supt Dr. Steve Ritter. Ridge View Elementary principal Melissa Marnholtz oversees PreK programming.

title: Principal, Harrisonville Early Childhood Center
notes: South KC metro Cass County district. ECC home to Little Cats Preschool. Dr. Anne Hickman also listed as Director per district handbook (secondary contact). Email convention verified: first.last@harrisonvilleschools.org.

title: Superintendent
notes: NE MO Marion County district (~1,155 students). MQPK (Missouri Quality Preschool) grant funded - serves eligible 4-yr-olds and free/reduced 3-yr-olds. Active EC enrollment/PAT recruitment. ECCe director name not public; superintendent appropriate decision-maker. Palmyra Elementary phone (573) 769-3736.

title: Superintendent
notes: Lincoln County district (~1,544 students PK-12). Serves Winfield, Chain of Rocks, Foley, Old Monroe. Williams became superintendent 2022. Smaller district - superintendent is primary curriculum/EC decision-maker. Test scores indicate room for academic improvement (24% math proficient).

title: Superintendent
notes: Callaway County district headquartered at Kingdom City. Auxvasse ES (PK-8) and Williamsburg ES (K-5) are elementary sites. New elementary school in development per 2024 Supt updates - construction/expansion signal. Thompson is current supt per 2024 Fulton Sun article.

title: Preschool and PAT Coordinator
notes: Very small Adair/Macon County district (~307 students K-12). Morris listed as Preschool and PAT staff. Single building K-12 setup. Email pattern inferred from district domain - low confidence on exact format.

title: Superintendent
notes: Randolph County district. Operates Early Childhood Learning Center (separate building, 660-269-8598). ECLC director name not surfaced publicly - need direct enrichment. Note Wright's email uses moberlyspartans.org (district uses both domains).

title: Superintendent
notes: Very small NW MO Nodaway County district headquartered in Graham. Single building K-12. Small enrollment makes superintendent appropriate EC decision-maker. Blackford community recently switched to Northwest district (2022).

title: Program Director, Chillicothe Early Learning Center
notes: NW MO Livingston County district. Chillicothe ELC at 900 Coach K St operates 8 preschool classes (half-day AM/PM, ~12 students each) plus infant/toddler daycare. Strong EC operation indicates active curriculum buyer.

title: Superintendent
notes: Very small Andrew County district in Cosby (NW MO). Single K-8 building (~200 students). Rebecca Grimes is K-8 Principal (bgrimes@avenuecityschool.org) - alternative contact. Small rural district structure.

title: Child Care Center Director
notes: Lafayette County KC east metro district. CCC on McQuerry campus serves infants through preschool in 5 age-based classrooms. Operates 6:45am-4:30pm Monday-Friday - full daycare model. Located at 402 W College St.

title: Superintendent
notes: Small Bates County district (~733 students PK-12 across 2 schools). 13:1 student-teacher ratio. Superintendent is primary curriculum decision-maker for small district.

title: Superintendent
notes: Small St. Clair County district. Miller confirmed via August 2024 board meeting minutes. Small rural district - superintendent decision-maker for EC/curriculum.

title: Superintendent
notes: Very small Ozark County district in southern MO. Single-building K-12 structure. Superintendent primary EC contact.

title: Superintendent
notes: Small Christian County district SW MO. District office at 118 W Mt Vernon, Billings. Small rural district - superintendent appropriate EC decision-maker.

title: Superintendent
notes: Stone/Taney County district near Branson. NEW LEADERSHIP: Alumbaugh started July 2024 from Pierce City. Three new principals hired same year - significant leadership transition cluster, potential signal for new curriculum decisions. Previous supt: Doug Arnold.

title: Superintendent
notes: Pike County NE MO district. NEW Early Childhood Center being added to district - active EC expansion signal. EC phone: 573-324-3550. New supt Larson (EdS) replaced Matt Frederickson, PhD. Strong curriculum buying signal with new ECC.

title: Superintendent
notes: Linn/Chariton County district. Operates Brookfield Bright Beginnings (Title I-funded preschool for ages 4-5 not kindergarten-eligible). Preschool phone 660-258-2445. Title I funding signals federal compliance focus.

title: Director of Special Education / Principal, Butler Early Childhood Center
notes: Bates County district (~1,035 students PK-12). Butler ECC at 300 S Delaware combines preschool and ECSE under SpEd directorship - notable district that bundles EC+SpEd under one leader. Erin Wainscott serves as EC Secretary.

title: Superintendent
notes: Bootheel Dunklin County district. NOTABLE: Board approved 4-day school week starting 2025-26 - rural staffing challenge signal. Foster active on teacher shortage advocacy. Small rural Title I-eligible community.

title: Superintendent
notes: Boone County district (~1,314 students PK-12). Chance Elementary (PK-2) houses preschool program at 123 N Rollins; PreK screening 573-682-2014. Active PAT program. District operates EC programs including Preschool, PAT, PAT Services, and Preschool Screening.

title: Superintendent
notes: Very small Moniteau County district. Single building K-12 setup. Bestgen serves as superintendent - primary EC decision-maker for the small rural district.

title: Principal, Clinton Early Childhood Center
notes: Henry County district. CECC at 502 S 5th St uses Project Construct curriculum framework. Licensed program with hands-on developmentally appropriate learning. District includes ECC, elementary, intermediate, middle, high, technical schools.

title: Superintendent
notes: Very small Cooper County district at Bunceton (also known as Bunceton R-IV). Single building rural district - superintendent decision-maker for EC/curriculum.

title: Superintendent
notes: SW MO Stone County district. NEW LEADERSHIP: Rogers promoted from Crane Elementary Principal (held since 2016) to Superintendent - strong elementary/EC background makes her a high-quality curriculum target. Recent transition signal.

title: Superintendent
notes: Very small Dade County district. Cassy Farmer listed as Principal. Single-building K-12 setup. Superintendent info from 2018-2019 - may need verification. Small rural district.

title: Superintendent
notes: Small rural Dent/Phelps County district near Salem. Brooker holds Superintendent K-12, Principal K-8, Library Media K-12, and Elementary Ed 1-6 certifications - strong elementary/EC background. Phone ext 1009 for direct line.

title: Superintendent
notes: Small Bates/Cass County KC south metro district. NEW SUPT: Gary French began July 2024 (board named Jan 2024) - leadership transition signal. Small rural district - superintendent primary EC decision-maker.

title: Superintendent
notes: Cedar/Vernon/St. Clair County district (~1,172 students PreK-12 across 3 schools). District holds Open House events at elementary and preschool buildings - dedicated preschool facility. Superintendent appropriate target.

title: Superintendent
notes: Very small Polk County district (~347 students PK-12, 3 schools). SUPT VACANCY 2025-26 per Missouri Association of Rural Education - active leadership transition signal. Buck Shockley also referenced in some sources. Application deadline Mar 21, 2025.
